title: Decider verify-push gap + queue archive-on-pop gap + analyst-baby dispatcher filter discipline + 4 zombie blocked-on-curmudgeon ISSs
background:
Four related discipline gaps surfaced in a single conversation today (2026-05-25 morning operator session). They share a pattern — agent dispatchers/writers skipping a downstream-handoff step — but they affect different agents (decider, analyst-baby) and different state-files (priority-queue + its archive, expansion-tracker, open-issues.json). Bundling them into one directive because the diagnostic work overlaps (same agents, same code paths) and a single tinker run can triage all four.

The trigger sequence: operator looked at the priority queue this morning expecting verify-class items, found queue empty. Investigation showed (a) decider 09:20Z run popped qid=402 (WIN-067.c13) and self-applied 2 minor patches but did NOT push a follow-up class='verification' item for WIN-067 c14 — contradicting decider.md L378 ('For both: when Step 5 self-apply pushes the patched target for re-review, set class: verification per PROP-025'); (b) priority-queue-archive.jsonl ends at qid=395 (popped 2026-05-21T06:05Z) but qids 396–402 were pushed and popped between 5/21 and 5/25 per tracker-archive.jsonl — the pop→archive-append step in PROP-022 Phase 3 has a gap; (c) analyst no-op'd EXP-425 for the 3rd consecutive run because analyst-baby dispatcher keeps surfacing it despite assigned_to:'tinker', and EXP-431 with status:'consolidated-into-EXP-432' may have the same surfacing problem; (d) operator search for 'blocked-on-curmudgeon' items found 4 ISSs whose underlying EXPs were integrated in April but ISSs never closed — ISS-1351 (EXP-211, integrated 2026-04-25), ISS-1552 (EXP-255→EXP-261, integrated 2026-04-27), ISS-1553 (EXP-256, integrated 2026-04-27), ISS-1599 (blocked on ISS-1605 dark-mode contrast — ISS-1605 already closed). None have routed_to_curmudgeon_queue_id, so they were never actually in the priority queue — they're zombie items in open-issues.json.

decider_verify_push_gap.summary_excerpt: Self-applied 2 minor patches to WIN-067... Queue: popped WIN-067 qid=402 (strict_queue_id); queue depth now 0
decider_verify_push_gap.expected_per_prompt: decider.md L378: 'For both: when Step 5 self-apply pushes the patched target for re-review, set class: verification per PROP-025.'
decider_verify_push_gap.actual_observed: next_id in priority-queue.json is 403 (push would have allocated 403), but queue array is [] and tracker-archive has no qid=403 entry. The verify-class push for WIN-067 c14 did not happen.
decider_verify_push_gap.c13_review_meta: monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/WIN-067.c13.json — 2 minor carry-over holes (code_analysis.monitoring=none + Mascon↔SAA redundancy), recommended_action=minor_edit, current_verdict_holds=true. Both holes within the PROP-038 Phase 1 gate (≤2 minor).
decider_verify_push_gap.downstream_effect: Both curmudgeon-verify (11:15Z) and main curmudgeon (11:47Z, fell back to drift-audit Priority 3 for WIN-020.c5) saw queue length 0 — correctly no-op'd / fell back. The empty queue is a downstream symptom of the missing decider push.

queue_archive_pop_gap.hypothesis: PROP-022 Phase 3 added a pop→archive-append step, but at least one decider pop path (likely the batched-pop or the strict_queue_id pop) is missing the append call. tracker-archive.jsonl captures reviewer-side metadata via curmudgeon's writer, but priority-queue-archive.jsonl is decider's responsibility on pop.

exp_425_state.target: Audit 86 missing EXP IDs in expansion tracker
exp_425_state.routing_reason: ISS-2105 requires audit of expansion-tracker gap ranges; does not affect published content but is a pipeline-integrity concern for tinker.

analyst_baby_dispatcher_filter_disciplin.observed_behavior: Analyst (Opus) has no-op'd on EXP-425 for 3 consecutive runs ('Counter-consideration on the EXP-425 surfacing... 3-run consecutive flagging is starting to look like noise the decider should suppress at source, not a real loop the analyst should solve.'). Each no-op is wasted Opus cost. EXP-431 may have the same pattern given status='consolidated-into-EXP-432' should signal 'don't pick up'.
analyst_baby_dispatcher_filter_disciplin.analyst_dual_hypothesis: (a) dispatcher script ignores assigned_to (operational bug) — favored, or (b) dispatcher is intentionally permissive and Opus is the classifier (overly broad). Either way, after 3 cycles the cost favors fixing the filter.

zombie_blocked_on_curmudgeon_iss.underlying_work: dark-mode contrast on EXP-256; blocked on ISS-1605

evidence.structural_insight: These four issues share a common shape: 'decider integrated EXP, set ISS to blocked-on-curmudgeon, did not push to curmudgeon queue, did not close ISS when EXP integration confirmed.' PROP-056's status='closed' migration sweep (landed today, commit da63f27f) doesn't catch them because their status is 'blocked-on-curmudgeon', not 'closed'. A second-pass burndown for blocked-on-curmudgeon ISSs whose source_exp is integrated would close them.

requested_work: Step 1 — Decider verify-push gap: Trace the decider self-apply path in monitor/prompts/reference/decider-patches-and-selfapply.md for the WIN-067 c13 case. Identify whether the verify-push step is conditional (e.g., suppressed when patches are 'trivial' or when current_verdict_holds=true) or whether it's unconditionally missing. If conditional, evaluate whether the condition is correct (Phase 1 PROP-038 gate says ≤2 minor holes + applied-patches → eligible for verify-class push, so this case should have pushed). If missing, identify the code path that pops the item but doesn't push the follow-up. Report findings and propose a one-line PROP if it's a prompt-level fix.

Step 2 — Queue archive-on-pop gap: Inspect the decider pop logic for the batched-pop and strict_queue_id pop paths. Both should append to priority-queue-archive.jsonl per PROP-022 Phase 3. Identify which path(s) are missing the fs.appendFileSync. Backfill the 7 missing entries (qids 396–402) from tracker-archive.jsonl metadata if feasible. Propose prompt fix.

Step 3 — Analyst-baby dispatcher: Read monitor/prompts/analyst-baby.md and locate the dispatcher filter that picks expansions from monitor/analyst/expansion-tracker.json. Verify it excludes items where assigned_to !== 'analyst' (or assigned_to in a deny-list) and where status indicates inactive (e.g., 'consolidated-into-*', 'complete', 'integrated'). If the filter is missing either guard, propose a prompt patch. Goal: stop surfacing EXP-425 (assigned to tinker) and EXP-431 (consolidated) to analyst.

Step 4 — Zombie ISS sweep design: Author a follow-up PROP (call it PROP-058 or whatever's next) that adds a decider intake step which sweeps open-issues.json for status='blocked-on-curmudgeon' AND source_exp.integrated=true (or equivalent: the referenced EXP file exists in tracker-archive with integrated:true). For each match, propose closing the ISS with closed_by_mechanism='exp-integrated-burndown'. Reference PROP-056 as the design template. Do NOT close the 4 known zombie ISSs in this directive run — the operator will pop the PROP through normal review.

Step 5 — Audit recurrence: Scan the last 14 days of decider runs (latest-decider-summary.txt + closure-ledger.jsonl) for the verify-push gap pattern: 'self-applied N minor patches' present in summary AND no subsequent push of class='verification' for the same target_id. List the targets that should have been pushed for re-review but weren't. This is the operational analog of Step 4 in DIRECTIVE-20260525-001 (the NEVER_PUSH false-closure audit) but for verify-cycle pushes.

Step 6 — Report: Summarize findings and proposals back via the standard tinker run report. If any fixes are one-line prompt patches, list them with file:line refs so operator can self-apply without a PROP round-trip. Larger fixes get PROPs.
non_goals: Do NOT batch-close the 4 zombie blocked-on-curmudgeon ISSs in this directive run — design the PROP first, operator decides whether to apply.
Do NOT modify decider.md or analyst-baby.md directly — propose changes via PROP if substantive.
Do NOT push qid=403 or any other queue items manually — the goal is to fix the discipline gap so decider does it itself on next run.
Do NOT touch the curmudgeon-side priority-queue audit (PROP-009r2) — that consumer reads the archive stream, but writing the missing entries is decider's job (Step 2).

context_pointers.directive_filed_alongside: DIRECTIVE-20260525-001 (PROP-016 Mech A NEVER_PUSH false-closure audit) — different failure mode (false-positive close vs. missing-push) but adjacent: both are decider discipline gaps in the close/route step.
claude_md_sections: File Ownership Rules > priority-queue.json (decider primary writer)
context_pointers.false_closure_audit_pattern_today: monitor/decisions/closed-issues.json entries with false_closure_audit block (ISS-2094, ISS-2102) — pattern is decider-self-apply against NEVER_PUSH targets. Step 5 here is the verify-push analog.

expected_output: Tinker run report covers: (a) decider verify-push gap diagnosis + proposed fix, (b) queue archive-on-pop gap diagnosis + proposed backfill + fix, (c) analyst-baby dispatcher filter audit + proposed fix, (d) zombie-ISS-sweep PROP authored, (e) Step 5 verify-push recurrence audit results.
estimated_effort: Mode 1 audit + small PROP authoring. ~25 min Opus.
